deck: Nora Lindström of Catholic Relief Services and Dimagi's Erin Quinn on moving global development past extractive data practices, returning insights to the communities they come from, and the evolving role of the iNGO in an era of localization.
seo_description: Nora Lindström of Catholic Relief Services on ending extractive data collection, returning data to communities, and the changing role of iNGOs in localization.
og_description: CRS's Nora Lindström on inclusive data sharing, localization, and centering the people behind every data point.

pull_quote.text: We as a sector have for a long time been very sort of extractive in our data practices. What I would like for us to do as a sector is to center those data subjects and really recognize their agency.
pull_quote.cite: Nora Lindström, on closing the data loop

bullets: Tech as an enabler, not the goal: why listening deeply to find the real problem has to come before any tool selection
From extractive to inclusive data: returning insights to community-embedded enumerators and village development committees so they can advocate for change
The Malawi example: how local committees used CRS data to successfully push for action on a community livestock issue
The localization tension: balancing shared-service efficiency against partner choice, double data entry, and aligning with country systems like DHIS2
The evolving iNGO: CRS shifting toward accompaniment, capacity strengthening, and preparing government partners to prime their own grants
Data literacy is the hard part: lessons from Madagascar and Senegal on making data meaningful, not just building a dashboard nobody can act on
Diverse teams win: why a social scientist leads CRS's ICT4D work and how cross-disciplinary backgrounds sharpen the ROI conversation

stats.label: countries where CRS operates, supported by a single global ICT4D team
projects using CommCare across CRS on any given day
active CommCare mobile users across CRS programs

guests.name: Nora Lindström
guests.role: Senior Director of ICT4D, leading a global team that supports technology for data collection and service delivery across CRS's programs worldwide.

guests.name: Erin Quinn
guests.role: Senior Director of Customer Success, working across iNGO partners on how teams adopt and adapt technology in the field.
